name: design-orchestration
description: Orchestrates design workflows by routing work through brainstorming, multi-agent review, and execution readiness in the correct order. Prevents premature implementation, skipped validation, and unreviewed high-risk designs.
capabilities: Routes design work through mandatory brainstorming, risk assessment, and execution phases
Enforces multi-agent review for high-risk designs
Blocks implementation without validated design approval
Classifies designs as low, moderate, or high risk based on defined factors
Maintains decision logs and validates required artifacts exist
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 23 high-severity and 6 medium-severity patterns. Manual review confirms these are FALSE POSITIVES: backticks are markdown code formatting for skill names, and 'cryptographic algorithm' mentions are YAML frontmatter misinterpreted by the scanner. This is a legitimate workflow orchestration skill with no actual code execution, network requests, or malicious patterns.
valueStatement: This skill ensures design ideas are properly brainstormed, reviewed by multiple agents, and validated before reaching implementation. It prevents costly mistakes by enforcing a structured workflow.
limitations: Does not generate designs itself - only orchestrates other skills
Requires external skills (brainstorming, multi-agent-brainstorming) to function
Does not execute implementation - only gates access to it
Depends on other skills providing required artifacts

useCases.title: Feature Design Governance
useCases.description: Apply structured review process to new feature designs before implementation begins

useCases.title: Risk-Aware Decision Making
useCases.description: Ensure high-risk system changes receive proper multi-agent validation

useCases.title: Design Quality Enforcement
useCases.description: Prevent premature implementation by requiring validated design artifacts

promptTemplates.title: New Feature Design Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to review the following feature proposal: [Describe your feature idea]. Follow the workflow: first invoke brainstorming if no design exists, then assess risk, and require multi-agent review for high-risk items.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new feature that needs design validation

promptTemplates.title: Existing Design Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to validate the attached design. Check for: Understanding Lock, Decision Log, design approval status. Block execution if any required artifacts are missing.
promptTemplates.scenario: Validating an existing design before implementation

promptTemplates.title: High-Risk Change Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to assess this change. Classify as low, moderate, or high risk based on user impact, irreversibility, operational cost, complexity, uncertainty, and novelty. Require multi-agent-brainstorming for high-risk items.
promptTemplates.scenario: Assessing risk level of a proposed change

promptTemplates.title: Execution Readiness Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Use design-orchestration to verify execution readiness. Confirm: design is approved, Decision Log is complete, major assumptions are documented, known risks are acknowledged. Block if conditions fail.
promptTemplates.scenario: Verifying all prerequisites before starting implementation

bestPractices: Always invoke this skill first when starting any design work to establish proper workflow
Complete all required artifacts (Understanding Lock, Decision Log) before attempting to proceed to next phases
Trust the risk classification and allow the skill to enforce appropriate review levels
antiPatterns: Attempting to skip required review phases for time savings
Bypassing this skill to go directly from idea to implementation
Providing incomplete design artifacts and expecting execution to proceed

faq.question: What is design-orchestration?
faq.answer: Design-orchestration is a meta-skill that controls the flow between other design skills. It ensures designs are properly brainstormed, reviewed, and validated before implementation begins.

faq.question: Does this skill generate designs?
faq.answer: No. This skill does not generate designs. It routes work to other skills (brainstorming, multi-agent-brainstorming) and enforces validation requirements.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill whenever you propose a new feature, system, or change where correctness matters more than speed, or when a design decision carries meaningful risk.

faq.question: What happens if I skip the required review?
faq.answer: The skill will block execution. Skipping required escalation or validation is prohibited. The workflow will return to the appropriate prior phase.

faq.question: How does risk classification work?
faq.answer: The skill classifies designs as low, moderate, or high risk based on factors including user impact, irreversibility, operational cost, complexity, uncertainty, and novelty.

faq.question: What artifacts must exist before execution?
faq.answer: Before execution, you need: approved design (single-agent or multi-agent), complete Decision Log, documented major assumptions, and acknowledged known risks.
